title: Apartment 402 Any Significance
wikitext:
[]: Apartment #402: Any Significance?             Quote Post by Man Chong » Tue Jan 25, 2005 9:20 pm Rei's apartment room number. Do you think it stands for something? Anything? or am I looking too too hard for symbols?

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Tue Jan 25, 2005 11:07 pm 402 = 6 Instinct dictates that the camera's occasional focus on that number suggests some kind of relevance, but I'm sure once it's discovered it will be less than amazing. Probably a throwaway reference to another anime, or something having to do with the Gainax's surrounding community or a staffer. Or just nothing at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jan 25, 2005 11:42 pm If you ignore the zero, it becomes 42, the most significant number in the universe!

[]:             Quote Post by Man Chong » Tue Jan 25, 2005 11:48 pm Reichu wrote: If you ignore the zero, it becomes 42, the most significant number in the universe!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jan 26, 2005 1:16 am Man Chong wrote: Um, explain? >.>

[]:             Quote Post by Man Chong » Wed Jan 26, 2005 1:20 am Reichu wrote: Man Chong wrote: Um, explain? >.>

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Wed Jan 26, 2005 6:51 am What was the number the computer gave on the angel analysis on ep5? Wasn't it also 402?

[]:             Quote Post by Rustÿ » Sun Jan 30, 2005 8:03 am isnt that from a hitchikers guide to the galaxy reichu? I havent read it myself but a lot of my friends have.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sun Jan 30, 2005 8:10 am AchtungAffen wrote: What was the number the computer gave on the angel analysis on ep5? Wasn't it also 402?

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Thu Feb 17, 2005 9:29 pm Another random though on this. What was Asuka's hospital room number in EoE?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Feb 17, 2005 10:50 pm DatDude wrote: Another random though on this. What was Asuka's hospital room number in EoE?

[]:             Quote Post by Sectre01 » Fri Feb 18, 2005 7:23 pm Reichu wrote: 303.

[]:             Quote Post by Hand of God » Sun Feb 20, 2005 7:17 pm 402=6 there are 3 numbers I think of it as 666 the anti-christ number 303=6 the same thing 601=7, 3 numbers, 777, God's number

[]:             Quote Post by Esion » Sun Feb 20, 2005 8:05 pm 7 is also refered to as the perfect number I do believe.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Mon Feb 21, 2005 10:05 am Hand of God wrote: 402=6 there are 3 numbers I think of it as 666 the anti-christ number 303=6 the same thing 601=7, 3 numbers, 777, God's number

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Mon Feb 21, 2005 12:45 pm So if 666 is evil, then why was it given the name of the firewall in EoE, is it cause its a pain to break?

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Mon Feb 21, 2005 1:05 pm Let's not forget the parking lot where Misato tried to talk some sense into Shinji. On the commentary they said that 667 was the "neighbor to the beast", whatever that means. As to the topic, the only reason I bugged Man Chong into posting it was because of another anime, Monster. In that show, a character just as creepy and cool (but a bit more evil ) as Rei is hospitalized in room 402. I thought that perhaps the directors of both shows were thinking the same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Feb 21, 2005 1:22 pm Sometimes numbers are significant, and at times they are very randomly chosen. A good example of numbers at play in Eva is Rei's name. Rei is a way of saying zero in Japanese. So it is fitting that Unit "Zero's" pilot's name would in fact be "Zero". For a non-Eva reference, X-Files creator Chris Carter would often focus on digital clocks with 11:12 on it. 11:12 was another way of rendering November 12th, his wife's birthdate. Given that Anno stated that he borrowed from everywhere, it is plausible that something like 402 may well have some obscure meaning to it, if perhaps only to the director. I get the impression that some sort of biographical research could turn it up.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Mon Feb 21, 2005 3:00 pm "Rei" is also one of the 7 codes of Bushido, "politeness".

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon Feb 21, 2005 7:40 pm I think we are grasping at straws here people. I say its just a number.
